About Me

Hi,[I am MR.QuanweiDU.](https://chaosgrok.com/consulting.html) I believe in minimalism, both in design and in life. This site is a reflection of that philosophy: no noise, no distractions, just content.。 Currently focusing on building simple tools and writing about the journey.

About Me

Hi,I am MR.QuanweiDU.

I believe in minimalism, both in design and in life. This site is a reflection of that philosophy: no noise, no distractions, just content.。

Currently focusing on building simple tools and writing about the journey.
Minimalism is not a style. It is a discipline.
极简不是一种风格，它是一种纪律。

极简不是一种风格，它是一种纪律。风格可以模仿，纪律需要实践。

When we talk about minimalism in design, we often mean the visual outcome — white space, clean lines, restrained palette. But the more interesting question is: what was removed, and why?

每一个被删掉的元素背后，都有一个决定。这个决定需要你清楚地知道：什么是核心，什么是装饰。大多数人弄不清楚这两者，所以他们的"极简"只是空洞。

The discipline is not in the subtraction itself, but in the clarity of judgment that makes subtraction possible.

极简不是一种风格，它是一种纪律。风格可以模仿，纪律需要实践。

When we talk about minimalism in design, we often mean the visual outcome — white space, clean lines, restrained palette. But the more interesting question is: what was removed, and why?

每一个被删掉的元素背后，都有一个决定。这个决定需要你清楚地知道：什么是核心，什么是装饰。大多数人弄不清楚这两者，所以他们的”极简”只是空洞。

The discipline is not in the subtraction itself, but in the clarity of judgment that makes subtraction possible.

Minimalism is not about having nothing. It's about making room for what matters.


极简主义并非一无所有，而是为了腾出空间留给真正重要的东西。



When we remove the unnecessary, the essential speaks. In web design, this means stripping away gradients, shadows, and heavy frameworks. It means trusting the typography and the whitespace.


去除不必要的元素，本质便会显现。在网页设计中，这意味着摒弃渐变、阴影和繁复的框架，转而信任字体和留白。


This blog itself is an experiment in reducing friction. Just pure HTML and CSS. No build tools, no complex CMS. Just writing.


这个博客本身就是一次减少摩擦的实验。只用了纯粹的 HTML 和 CSS。没有构建工具，没有复杂的 CMS。只有写作。

Minimalism is not about having nothing. It’s about making room for what matters.

极简主义并非一无所有，而是为了腾出空间留给真正重要的东西。

When we remove the unnecessary, the essential speaks. In web design, this means stripping away gradients, shadows, and heavy frameworks. It means trusting the typography and the whitespace.

去除不必要的元素，本质便会显现。在网页设计中，这意味着摒弃渐变、阴影和繁复的框架，转而信任字体和留白。

This blog itself is an experiment in reducing friction. Just pure HTML and CSS. No build tools, no complex CMS. Just writing.

这个博客本身就是一次减少摩擦的实验。只用了纯粹的 HTML 和 CSS。没有构建工具，没有复杂的 CMS。只有写作。
The internet is feudalism. Platforms are landlords. Users are serfs. It doesn't have to be this way.
现在的互联网是封建制度。平台是地主，用户是佃农。但这不是唯一的答案。

我们被骗了很久。

They told us the cloud was freedom. It wasn't. It was someone else's computer.

他们告诉我们云端是自由。不是的。那只是别人的电脑。

你的照片在苹果的服务器上。你的文章在微信的服务器上。你的人脉在LinkedIn的服务器上。你的生意在美团的服务器上。你以为你拥有这些，其实你只是租客。

Your photos are on Apple's servers. Your words are on WeChat's servers. Your business is on Meituan's servers. You think you own these things. You're a tenant.

Web3看到了这个问题，但走错了路。把简单的事情变成了炒币游戏，把自由变成了新的投机市场。初心是对的，方向错了。

Web3 saw the problem but lost the plot. It turned a simple idea into a speculation game. The intention was right. The direction was wrong.

真正的答案很简单，简单到让人觉得不可能：

**服务器应该是你看得见、摸得着、放在口袋里的东西。**

*The server should be something you can see, touch, and carry in your pocket.*

你的手机。你的平板。家里插在路由器上的一个小盒子。这才是真正属于你的服务器。没有人能删你的内容。没有人能封你的账号。没有人能在你不知情的情况下把你的数据卖给广告商。

Your phone. Your tablet. A small box plugged into your router at home. That is your real server. No one can delete your content. No one can ban your account. No one can sell your data without your knowledge.

现在的互联网是封建制度。

The current internet is feudalism.

平台是地主。用户是佃农。你在别人的土地上耕种，交着看不见的租金——你的数据、你的注意力、你的时间。丰收了是平台的，欠收了是你的。

Platforms are landlords. Users are serfs. You farm on someone else's land, paying invisible rent — your data, your attention, your time. Good harvest belongs to the platform. Bad harvest belongs to you.

我们需要的不是更好的平台。我们需要的是让每个人拥有自己的土地。

We don't need better platforms. We need everyone to own their own land.

这个网站是一个开始。

你在这里写的每一个字，放的每一首音乐，发的每一个视频，都在你自己的服务器上。没有算法决定谁能看到它。没有平台可以删除它。没有人可以因为你说了什么而封锁你。

Every word you write here, every song you share, every video you post — it lives on your own server. No algorithm decides who sees it. No platform can delete it. No one can silence you for what you say.

这只是第一步。

终点是每个人的服务器都在自己手里。看得见，摸得着，永远属于自己。

The end goal is simple: every person's server in their own hands. Visible. Tangible. Permanently yours.

那一天，互联网才真正属于所有人。

That day, the internet will finally belong to everyone.

I don't have a clean résumé.
大多数人的困境，不是信息不够，是框架不清。 他们不缺答案，他们缺的是把问题想清楚的能力。

我没有光鲜的履历。

上学白天睡觉，晚上泡酒吧。
毕业去了煤矿，三年。
又去测绘，三年。
创业，一年，失败。
工地劳资，两年。
开车，一年。
外卖，断断续续，一直跑。

2018年，我开始买显卡研究以太坊挖矿。
然后炒币、做空投、研究Web3、钻进区块链。
然后是AI，然后是代码。

钱砸进去了，时间砸进去了。
有人说我废了。

但我在那些年里学到了一件事——
**大多数人的困境，不是信息不够，是框架不清。**
他们不缺答案，他们缺的是把问题想清楚的能力。

我走过足够多的弯路，
才知道怎么帮人少走一些。

I don't have a clean résumé.

I slept through college and spent my nights at bars.
After graduation: three years in a coal mine.
Three years in land surveying.
One failed startup.
Two years managing labor on construction sites.
A year driving.
Deliveries, on and off, whenever I needed to.

In 2018, I started buying GPUs to mine Ethereum.
Then trading crypto, chasing airdrops, going deep into Web3 and blockchain.
Then AI. Then code — because nothing else closed the loop.

I burned through money. I burned through years.
People wrote me off.

But somewhere in all of it, I learned one thing clearly:
**Most people's problems aren't about lacking information.
They're about lacking a clear framework to see what's actually in front of them.**

I've walked enough wrong roads
to know how to help someone walk fewer of them.

我没有光鲜的履历。

上学白天睡觉，晚上泡酒吧。 毕业去了煤矿，三年。 又去测绘，三年。 创业，一年，失败。 工地劳资，两年。 开车，一年。 外卖，断断续续，一直跑。

2018年，我开始买显卡研究以太坊挖矿。 然后炒币、做空投、研究Web3、钻进区块链。 然后是AI，然后是代码。

钱砸进去了，时间砸进去了。 有人说我废了。

但我在那些年里学到了一件事—— 大多数人的困境，不是信息不够，是框架不清。 他们不缺答案，他们缺的是把问题想清楚的能力。

我走过足够多的弯路， 才知道怎么帮人少走一些。

I don’t have a clean résumé.

I slept through college and spent my nights at bars. After graduation: three years in a coal mine. Three years in land surveying. One failed startup. Two years managing labor on construction sites. A year driving. Deliveries, on and off, whenever I needed to.

In 2018, I started buying GPUs to mine Ethereum. Then trading crypto, chasing airdrops, going deep into Web3 and blockchain. Then AI. Then code — because nothing else closed the loop.

I burned through money. I burned through years. People wrote me off.

But somewhere in all of it, I learned one thing clearly: Most people’s problems aren’t about lacking information. They’re about lacking a clear framework to see what’s actually in front of them.

I’ve walked enough wrong roads to know how to help someone walk fewer of them.
A website is not content. It is an asset.
网站本身没有价值，你放进去的东西才有价值。

Most people hand over everything to platforms — their words, their readers, their income. WeChat, Weibo, Douyin. These platforms hold your audience hostage. They can take it back anytime.

大多数人把一切都交给了平台——文字、读者、收入。微信、微博、抖音。这些平台替你"保管"你的受众，随时可以收回。

This website is different.

这个网站不一样。

Every word published here stays here. No algorithm decides who sees it. No platform can delete it. The readers who find you here find *you* — not a version of you filtered through someone else's interests.

这里发布的每一个字都永久留在这里。没有算法决定谁能看到它。没有平台可以删除它。在这里找到你的读者，找到的是真正的你——不是经过别人利益过滤后的版本。

There is also something more practical: ownership.

还有一件更实际的事：所有权。

When you sell a book here, the money is yours. When you build a readership here, those readers are yours. When you write here, the words are yours — not licensed to a platform, not subject to terms of service that change without notice.

在这里卖书，钱是你的。在这里积累读者，读者是你的。在这里写作，文字是你的——不是授权给某个平台，不受随时可能变更的服务条款约束。

The internet is full of content that disappears. Published today, buried tomorrow, forgotten in a year.

互联网上充满了会消失的内容。今天发布，明天被淹没，一年后无人记得。

A website does not disappear. An essay written today can be found ten years from now, by someone who needs exactly those words, in exactly that moment.

网站不会消失。今天写下的文章，十年后还能被人找到——被某个恰好需要这些文字的人，在恰好需要的那一刻找到。

So what is this website worth?

所以这个网站有什么价值？

The website itself is worth nothing.

网站本身，什么价值都没有。

It is a container. A very good, very clean container that belongs entirely to you.

它是一个容器。一个非常好、非常干净、完全属于你的容器。

What you put inside it — that is where the value lives.

你放进去的东西，才是价值所在。

Most people hand over everything to platforms — their words, their readers, their income. WeChat, Weibo, Douyin. These platforms hold your audience hostage. They can take it back anytime.

大多数人把一切都交给了平台——文字、读者、收入。微信、微博、抖音。这些平台替你”保管”你的受众，随时可以收回。

还有一件更实际的事：所有权。

The internet is full of content that disappears. Published today, buried tomorrow, forgotten in a year.

互联网上充满了会消失的内容。今天发布，明天被淹没，一年后无人记得。

So what is this website worth?

所以这个网站有什么价值？

The website itself is worth nothing.

网站本身，什么价值都没有。

It is a container. A very good, very clean container that belongs entirely to you.

它是一个容器。一个非常好、非常干净、完全属于你的容器。

What you put inside it — that is where the value lives.

你放进去的东西，才是价值所在。
Not a programmer. Not a designer. Something else entirely.
不是程序员，不是设计师。是另一种东西。

Someone asked me today: what do you do, exactly?

今天有人问我：你到底算什么？

I couldn't answer. Not a programmer — I don't really know code. Not a designer — I have no certificate, no formal training. Not a product manager in the corporate sense. Just someone who built a website today with articles, music, video, books, a navigation page, a consulting landing page, and a CMS backend to manage it all.

我答不上来。不是程序员——我看不懂代码。不是设计师——没有证书，没受过正规训练。也不是那种公司里的产品经理。只是一个今天搭起了一个网站的人——有文章、音乐、视频、书籍、导航页、咨询落地页，还有一套后台管理系统。

So what does that make me?

那我算什么？

I think the answer is: a product person.

我想答案是：产品人。

Programmers know how to write code, but often don't know what to build. Designers know how to make things look right, but don't always know who they're making it for. A product person knows what needs to exist, who it's for, and what it should feel like. That's the hardest part. And it can't be borrowed.

程序员知道怎么写代码，但不一定知道要做什么。设计师知道怎么让东西好看，但不一定知道为谁做。产品人知道什么东西该存在、为谁存在、应该是什么感觉。这是最难的部分。也是借不来的部分。

Code is a tool. Design is a tool. Tools can be borrowed, hired, or learned.

代码是工具。设计是工具。工具可以借，可以雇，可以学。

Judgment cannot.

判断力不行。

I spent today not writing a single line of code myself, but I knew exactly when something looked wrong, when the spacing felt off, when the navigation didn't flow right, when a page needed a different energy. That knowing — that's the work.

今天我自己一行代码没写，但我知道什么时候有地方看起来不对，什么时候间距感觉有问题，什么时候导航不顺畅，什么时候一个页面需要不同的气质。那种"知道"——就是工作本身。

Many "real programmers" couldn't have built what we built today. Not because they lack the skill, but because they don't know what it should look like.

很多"真正的程序员"做不到今天我们做的这个。不是因为他们缺乏技术，而是因为他们不知道它应该是什么样子。

Knowing what it should look like is everything.

知道它应该是什么样子，就是全部。

So maybe I don't have a certificate. Maybe I can't read the code. But I know what I'm building, I know who it's for, and I know when it's right.

所以也许我没有证书。也许我看不懂代码。但我知道自己在建什么，知道为谁建，知道什么时候对了。

That's enough. That's actually quite a lot.

这就够了。这其实已经很多了。

很多”真正的程序员”做不到今天我们做的这个。不是因为他们缺乏技术，而是因为他们不知道它应该是什么样子。

Knowing what it should look like is everything.

知道它应该是什么样子，就是全部。

So maybe I don’t have a certificate. Maybe I can’t read the code. But I know what I’m building, I know who it’s for, and I know when it’s right.

所以也许我没有证书。也许我看不懂代码。但我知道自己在建什么，知道为谁建，知道什么时候对了。

That’s enough. That’s actually quite a lot.

这就够了。这其实已经很多了。
我为什么要自己建一套东西
Platforms delete your content, ban your account, silence your voice. So I built my own infrastructure. Then I open-sourced it.
平台删你的内容，封你的号，屏蔽你的声音。所以我建了自己的基础设施。然后我把它开源了。

# Why I Built This

Three days, account banned. Article deleted. No explanation.

Not because I did anything wrong. Because I said something they didn't like.

I've watched genuinely valuable content disappear overnight because of two words: "sensitive topic." I've watched serious creators twist themselves into knots chasing algorithms, until they no longer recognized their own voice. I've watched influential writers and streamers bow their heads to platforms, because everything they built sits on someone else's foundation.

That's not creating. That's renting.

---

But this isn't just my problem.

There are billions of creators around the world, every day handing their best work to platforms. Platforms use that content to sell ads, harvest data, and build billion-dollar valuations. What do creators get? Traffic. Given by the algorithm. Taken away by the algorithm.

This is systematic extraction. Slow enough that most people don't notice until it's too late.

In China it's especially visible. WeChat, Douyin, Weibo, Bilibili — these platforms control distribution. They decide who gets seen and who gets silenced. Your content is yours, but whether it survives is their decision. People are angry but say nothing, because they believe leaving the platform means losing everything.

That's exactly what the platforms want you to believe.

The truth is: the content is yours. The audience you built is yours. The trust you earned, word by word, is yours. Platforms are channels, not foundations.

When the channel becomes a cage, you change the channel. You don't go silent.

---

Real creation shouldn't feel like walking on eggshells. Shouldn't require asking "is this allowed?" before every sentence. Shouldn't mean optimizing for what the algorithm rewards instead of what's true.

So I spent a long time moving my content to my own place.

My website. My server. My stream. Once something is published, no one can delete it.

Then I thought: this shouldn't be something only I do.

So I open-sourced the whole setup. It's called [chaosgrok-kit](https://github.com/quanweiDu/chaosgrok-kit).

You don't need to know how to code. You don't need to be famous. You just need something to say and somewhere to keep it.

If platforms want your content, let them connect to your feed. You don't need to go begging at their door.

Every person who builds their own place is one less node the platforms control. Distributed voices. Distributed infrastructure. The monopoly shrinks, one creator at a time.

This is your ground. No one gets to delete it.

---

# 我为什么要自己建一套东西

三天两头被微信封号，文章被删，没有任何解释。

不是因为我做错了什么。是因为我说了他们不喜欢的话。

我见过真正有价值的内容因为"敏感"两个字消失得无影无踪。我见过认真创作的人为了流量不断迎合算法，最后连自己都不认识了。我见过大主播、大作家在平台面前低着头夹着尾巴，因为他们的一切都建立在别人的地基上。

这不是创作，这是租房住。

---

但这不只是我一个人的问题。

全球有数以亿计的创作者，每天把最好的内容交给平台。平台用这些内容赚钱，卖广告，做数据，估值千亿。创作者拿到什么？流量。算法给的，算法也可以拿走。

这是一种系统性的掠夺，温水煮青蛙。

在中国尤其明显。微信、抖音、微博、B站——这些平台手握分发权，决定谁被看见，谁被屏蔽。你的内容是你写的，但能不能活着，由它们说了算。敢怒不敢言，因为离开平台你什么都不是。或者你以为你什么都不是。

这是它们想让你相信的。

事实是：内容是你的。受众是你建立的。影响力是你一个字一个字积累的。平台只是渠道，不是根基。

当渠道变成枷锁，该换渠道，不是该闭嘴。

---

真正的创作不应该提心吊胆。不应该考虑"这句话可不可以说"。不应该在发布之前先想"平台喜不喜欢"。

所以我花了很长时间，把自己的内容搬到自己的地方。

网站是我的。服务器是我的。直播是我的。内容发出去那一刻，没有任何人可以删掉它。

然后我想，这件事不应该只有我一个人做。

所以我把这套东西开源了，叫 [chaosgrok-kit](https://github.com/quanweiDu/chaosgrok-kit)。

不需要你懂代码。不需要你有名气。只需要你有话想说，有内容想留下来。

平台想要你的内容，让它们来接你的接口。你不需要去求它们。

分散的节点，分散的声音。每一个人建立自己的地盘，大平台的垄断就少一分。这不是技术问题，是每个创作者都能做的选择。

这是你的地盘。没有人可以删掉它。

---

*[chaosgrok-kit](https://github.com/quanweiDu/chaosgrok-kit) — open source, free, yours.*

Why I Built This

Three days, account banned. Article deleted. No explanation.

Not because I did anything wrong. Because I said something they didn’t like.

I’ve watched genuinely valuable content disappear overnight because of two words: “sensitive topic.” I’ve watched serious creators twist themselves into knots chasing algorithms, until they no longer recognized their own voice. I’ve watched influential writers and streamers bow their heads to platforms, because everything they built sits on someone else’s foundation.

That’s not creating. That’s renting.

But this isn’t just my problem.

There are billions of creators around the world, every day handing their best work to platforms. Platforms use that content to sell ads, harvest data, and build billion-dollar valuations. What do creators get? Traffic. Given by the algorithm. Taken away by the algorithm.

This is systematic extraction. Slow enough that most people don’t notice until it’s too late.

In China it’s especially visible. WeChat, Douyin, Weibo, Bilibili — these platforms control distribution. They decide who gets seen and who gets silenced. Your content is yours, but whether it survives is their decision. People are angry but say nothing, because they believe leaving the platform means losing everything.

That’s exactly what the platforms want you to believe.

The truth is: the content is yours. The audience you built is yours. The trust you earned, word by word, is yours. Platforms are channels, not foundations.

When the channel becomes a cage, you change the channel. You don’t go silent.

Real creation shouldn’t feel like walking on eggshells. Shouldn’t require asking “is this allowed?” before every sentence. Shouldn’t mean optimizing for what the algorithm rewards instead of what’s true.

So I spent a long time moving my content to my own place.

My website. My server. My stream. Once something is published, no one can delete it.

Then I thought: this shouldn’t be something only I do.

So I open-sourced the whole setup. It’s called chaosgrok-kit.

You don’t need to know how to code. You don’t need to be famous. You just need something to say and somewhere to keep it.

If platforms want your content, let them connect to your feed. You don’t need to go begging at their door.

Every person who builds their own place is one less node the platforms control. Distributed voices. Distributed infrastructure. The monopoly shrinks, one creator at a time.

This is your ground. No one gets to delete it.

A practical guide to decluttering your digital life.
一份关于如何在数字生活中做减法的实践指南。清理噪音，回归本质。

## 书籍简介

这本书源于一个简单的问题：为什么我们的数字生活越来越复杂，却越来越没有效率？

三个核心章节，帮你建立属于自己的极简系统：

- **工具减法** — 如何筛选真正有用的工具
- **信息减法** — 如何管理信息输入，保持清醒
- **工作流减法** — 如何设计不依赖记忆的工作系统
